title: Dashboard / LOD / Rendering
description: SVG → Canvas → WebGL → WebGPU → Vulkan substrate ladder for visualizing the gsd-skill-creator codebase + planning docs as an interactive, navigable artifact. Ships a working LOD demo rendering the 32-node T5 sample-provenance graph.

self_demo.corpus: 32-node T5 sample-provenance graph + synthesized 1K-file slice (stretch corpus button)
self_demo.substrate_handoff: SVG ↔ Canvas verified live (Auto button + manual SVG/Canvas toggle)

substrate_decisions.decision: SVG → Canvas → WebGL → WebGPU → Tauri+wgpu substrate ladder
Cross-substrate semantic identity via T5 prov_node.node_id
sub_type as visual-styling discriminator across all renderers
WGSL compute kernels (force-atlas-2.wgsl) compile unchanged for browser WebGPU AND native Vulkan/Metal/DX12 via wgpu
Lazy-redraw pattern (needsRedraw flag); 3 instanced draws per frame at WebGL rung
Picking buffer (not spatial-index) for WebGL hit-testing
Selected nodes pinned during force-directed layout iteration
Four query primitives — LOOKUP / NEIGHBOURHOOD / SEARCH / HYBRID-SEARCH — all backed by T5 substrate

files.webgl-primitives/edge-batch.glsl: Wide-line edge primitive via per-instance quad extrusion
files.webgl-primitives/regl-setup.ts: regl declarative wrapper showing the bind shape
files.webgpu-compute/force-atlas-2.wgsl: Three FA2 compute kernels (repulsion + attraction + integration)
files.dashboard-service/serve.mjs: Minimal Node HTTP server (static + optional PG proxy)
files.dashboard-service/package.json: Service package (zero required deps; pg optional)
files.dashboard/app.js: Pure-ESM dashboard app (renderer-router, layout, query, inspector)
files.dashboard/viewer-embed.js: CAP-041 T3↔T4 viewer-embed module — openViewerFor/closeViewer/isViewerOpen + sandboxed iframe lifecycle

papers: Jacomy, Venturini, Heymann, Bastian (2014). ForceAtlas2, PLOS ONE.
Brendel et al. (2024). WebGPU for Graphs, IEEE VIS.
Sugiyama, Tagawa, Toda (1981). Methods for Visual Understanding of Hierarchical System Structures, IEEE SMC.
Hu (2005). Efficient and high quality force-directed graph drawing, Mathematica Journal.
Buja, McDonald, Michalak, Stuetzle (1991). Interactive Data Visualization Using Focusing and Linking, IEEE Visualization.
Green (2007). Improved Alpha-Tested Magnification for Vector Textures, SIGGRAPH.
Shneiderman (1991). Tree-maps: A space-filling approach, IEEE Visualization.
Purchase (2002). Metrics for Graph Drawing Aesthetics, J. Visual Languages & Computing.
